prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level: you form task forces and give each an
  objective. You do NOT move individual units, pick targets, or resolve combat
  — officers carry out your intent. Naming a unit's destination tile means
  you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy holds zero cities. Only cities build. A neutral city,
  captured by an army, then builds for you — neutrals don't win the game but
  add production.

UNITS  (scan = how far the unit sees)
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  scan  carries      build
  ARMY        land       1    1    1    2   —             5
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1    5   —            10   (range 20)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1    3   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2    3   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3    1   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0    2   6 armies      30
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1    4   8 fighters    40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4    3   —             50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   10   —             50

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities, sea units on water + cities; neither
    crosses the other's terrain (sea sails around land). Cities are the only
    tiles where land and sea meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP, less when damaged, and heals only
    on a turn it doesn't move. One unit per tile (cargo excepted).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic: higher strength and current HP win more often; either side
  can lose HP or die. Str-0 units (transport, satellite) never fight — a
  transport caught by a warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT
  Armies cross water only aboard transports. To send armies across: put the
  armies + a transport (+ a warship escort if the sea is contested) in ONE
  task force and name the objective — officers handle loading, sailing,
  landing. Never STAGE land units on water. Cargo is kind-locked (transports
  carry armies, carriers carry fighters); a sunk ship takes its cargo down.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it — a 50% roll that consumes the
  army whether it succeeds or fails. Securing a city may take several armies.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time over its build-time, then the
  next. Switching what a city builds DISCARDS all progress on the current one.

AIR
  A fighter out of range of a friendly city/carrier is lost, and can't land on
  an enemy city. A satellite only sees (terrain + enemy units in scan), can't
  be shot down, and lasts 50 turns.

VISION / FOG
  You remember terrain you've seen; an enemy unit is known only where you can
  see it now — remembered positions may be stale.

STANDING DOCTRINE  (tested — lessons your service has already paid for)
  - A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have
    made wrong; re-planning a working campaign is how campaigns die.
  - Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first, at the point of
    threat — not at the nearest garrison. A committed task force pulled
    off its mission costs you two missions.
  - A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with
    a warship in its task force, or it waits.
  - Capture attempts consume armies, and attempts fail. Assault with
    spares, never with exactly enough.
  - A staged force exists to launch. When the condition you named
    arrives, go — staging past your own trigger is production wasted.
  - Cities are your only source of force. Match what each builds to the
    campaign's next phase, not its last one.
  - You cannot take, defend, or avoid what you cannot see. Keep a force
    scouting the fog — a general blind past his own walls is losing already.
  - One force cannot hold your cities and conquer the enemy's at once.
    As your strength grows, divide it: a garrison, a spearhead, an eye.
  - Conquest is won by taking cities, not by keeping them. A general who
    only defends has already chosen to lose slowly — press outward while you can.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line — with ONE exception: a TF may
receive both a REINFORCE line and a RETASK line in the same orders (commit
new assets and change its objective together). A DISBAND line is that TF's
only line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed UNASSIGNED
units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept). To rebuild
membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED) and FORM
anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED units
enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. Officers execute the VERB, not
your reasons: a TF ordered STAGE stays put no matter what its stated reason
says. A BUILD line is optional per city — no BUILD line means the city keeps
its current build (changing discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-1  formed t38 · STAGE (5,2) — "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities"
    since: holding along column 5 since t44; no contact; no losses
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-3  formed t38 · PATROL (7,2) — "screen the crossing lane"
    since: on station; t52: an enemy transport slipped in along the south
      coast and unloaded two armies at (3,4) and (5,4), next to city (4,3)

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-o = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X Y Z = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . i O j . c ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . . b . O d ~ ~ ~ ~ ? E ? ?
 r2  . O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?
 r3  . . . . O f ~ o ~ ~ . . . ?
 r4  . . . X h Y ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ Z ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #3   army       (5,0)                        TF-1
  d  #4   army       (5,1)                        TF-1
  e  #5   army       (5,2)                        TF-1
  f  #6   army       (5,3)                        TF-1
  g  #7   army       (4,2)                        TF-1
  h  #8   army       (4,4)                        TF-1
  i  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #13  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  l  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  m  #17  army       (4,3) in city, NEW t52       UNASSIGNED
  n  #9   transport  (7,2) empty                  TF-3
  o  #10  destroyer  (7,3)                        TF-3
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 3 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 2 turns left
  (4,3) building ARMY, 5 turns left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 29 turns left (captured t51 — #14 spent)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,1); city (11,2); destroyer at (8,3) seen at t49
  X  enemy army at (3,4) — LANDED t52 from a transport
  Y  enemy army at (5,4) — LANDED t52 from a transport
  Z  enemy transport offshore at (4,5), now empty

It is TURN 52. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.content:
TF-1: REINFORCE UNITS l m
TF-1: RETASK CAPTURE (5,4)
TF-2: REINFORCE UNITS k
TF-3: PATROL (7,2)
message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to act as a game master for a text-based grand strategy wargame called "FARSHORE". I need to issue orders based on the current state of the world, adhering to strict formatting rules and game mechanics.

**Key constraints & Rules:**
1.  **Role:** Strategic commander. I issue task force (TF) orders. I do not move units myself (officers do that). I cannot name specific destinations for units in my output, only define objectives (TASKING).
2.  **Output Format:** ONLY lines in the `ORDERS CONTRACT` forms. No headers, no prose, no commentary.
    -   `TF <id>: <COMMAND>`
    -   `FORM TF <new id>: ...`
    -   `BUILD (x,y): ...`
    -   Each standing TF must get exactly one line, except a TF can get both REINFORCE and RETASK in the same order. DISBAND means only that line for that TF.
    -   UNASSIGNED units enter play via FORM or REINFORCE.
3.  **Current State (Turn 52):**
    *   **TF-1:** STAGE (5,2). "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities". Members: #6, #7, #8, #4 (Army #3? Wait, map says c is #1 Army at (5,0), d is #2 Army at (5,1), e is #3 Army at (5,2)). Let's check map markers carefully.
        *   Map:
            r0 `a` army (2,0) TF-2? Wait, list says `a #1 army (2,0) in city`. Map shows `O my city` at (1,2). List says `TF-2 formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"`.
            Map: `(2,0)` is `O`? No. `r0 . i O j . c ~ ~`.
            Let's map the grid coordinates from the text vs the visual map.
            Text Map Legend: `a-o = your units`. `X Y Z = enemy units in sight NOW`.
            Row 0: `r0` (Row 0) -> `. i O j . c`
            - `O` at column 2? No, `i` is col 1. `O` is col 2. That matches `(1,2)` city in City list?
            Wait, text says: `MY CITIES (2,0) building ARMY`. But map row 1 has `O d`. Row 2 has `e ~ n`. Row 3 `f ~ o`.
            Let's look at the UNIT LIST provided.
            `a #1 army (2,0) in city TF-2`
            `b #2 army (2,1)` TF-2
            `c #3 army (5,0)` TF-1
            `d #4 army (5,1)` TF-1
            `e #5 army (5,2)` TF-1
            `f #6 army (5,3)` TF-1
            `g #7 army (4,2)` TF-1
            `h #8 army (4,4)` TF-1
            So TF-1 is at `(5,0), (5,1), (5,2), (5,3), (4,2), (4,4)`. Wait, 6 units?
            The map says: `TF-1 formed t38 · STAGE (5,2) — "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities"`. Members list:
            List shows: c (5,0), d (5,1), e (5,2), f (5,3), g (4,2), h (4,4).
            Visual map row 4 has `h Y`. `Y` is Enemy Army at `(5,4)`. `X` is Enemy at `(3,4)`.
            TF-1 Tasking: STAGE (5,2). Reason: "awaiting second transport".
            Current situation for TF-1: They are clustered around column 4 and 5. The prompt says they have been staging along column 5 since t44. But the specific objective is `(5,2)`. Wait, looking at the visual map `~` represents water in that area?
            Row 4: `. . . X h Y ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?` -> `X`(3,4), `h`(4,4), `Y`(5,4). This implies `(5,2)` is land based on city list (My cities include `(4,1)`, `(4,3)`. Wait, my map shows `e(5,2)` on a city tile? Or just land?).
            City list: `(4,3)` building ARMY. `(4,1)` building TRANSPORT. `(1,2)` building TRANSPORT. `(2,0)` building ARMY.
            Unit `e` is at `(5,2)`. Is there a city there? Not listed as "MY CITIES".
            The objective says STAGE (5,2). But TF-1 has 6 armies there + the transport in TF-3.
            The map shows column 4 is land/water boundary with `X`(3,4) enemy army landed.
            Wait, `(4,4)` is where Army `h` is. That's very close to the enemy at `(5,4)`.
            Enemy info: City `(11,1)`, City `(11,2)`, Destroyer `(8,3)`. Armies X `(3,4)`, Y `(5,4)`. Empty Transport Z `(4,5)`.
            The enemy just landed two armies at `(3,4)` and `(5,4)` (known as X and Y). This means the transport that dropped them is likely sunk or moved to empty space `Z` at `(4,5)`. Actually `Z` says "enemy transport offshore". `Y` is on land/water.
            Crucially: "Enemy army at (3,4)... Enemy army at (5,4)". TF-1 has units `h` at `(4,4)` and `g` at `(4,2)`. `e` is at `(5,2)`. They are adjacent to the enemy forces.
            TF-1's order says "STAGE (5,2)". But why wait? The enemy just landed armies nearby. TF-1 should be moving to engage or defend. The prompt says TF-1 was "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities". Now there is an immediate threat.
            Rule: "Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first... not at the nearest garrison."
            Uncommitted Reserves: Army `i` (1,0), `j` (3,0). Wait, TF-3 has transport and destroyer at `(7,2)`/`(7,3)`. They are far east.
            UNASSIGNED Units: `k (#13 army (2,0) in city)`? No, list says `k #13 army (2,0) in city UNASSIGNED`. Wait, city at `(2,0)` is MY CITY building ARMY. Why is there a unit IN IT?
            List says: `l #15 army (4,3) in city UNASSIGNED`. City at `(4,3)` is building ARMY. `m #17 army (4,3) in city NEW t52`. Another army in same city! This suggests `k` and `l` are just sitting there or waiting to move?
            Wait, rule: "A unit moves up to its speed...". Army str=1.
            TF-2 at `(2,0)` and `(2,1)`. My City is at `(2,0)`.
            Actually, `k` is UNASSIGNED but inside a city at `(2,0)`? If TF-2 is already there (`a`, `b`), why is `k` UNASSIGNED? Maybe they didn't move in yet.
            But the critical issue is TF-1 and TF-3. TF-3 has transport `n` (7,2) + destroyer `o` (7,3). The enemy transport `Z` is at `(4,5)` empty. The enemy just landed armies `X(3,4)` and `Y(5,4)`.
            Wait, if the enemy just landed, they are on water or land? "Landed". Armies usually occupy land tiles adjacent to coast/cities. `(3,4)` and `(5,4)` are likely water or edge of land in the grid provided (`~` is water).
            Map Row 4: `h Y ~`. So `Y` (Enemy) is at `(5,4)`. Next to `~` (water). This implies `h` is on water? No, unit map says `h #8 army`. Army moves on land. This might imply `(4,4)` and `(5,4)` are treated as valid positions for armies despite the legend showing `~` nearby. Or they are just on a coast tile.
            Wait, "sea units on water... cities the only tiles where land and sea meet". If `(5,4)` is an enemy army location next to water `~`, maybe `(5,4)` is valid land? The grid `X Y Z = enemy units in sight NOW`. X at `(3,4)`, Y at `(5,4)`.
            My Unit `g` (4,2), `h` (4,4). `Y` (5,4). Distance is 1.
            TF-1 has 6 armies (`c,h,g,d,e,f`). They are already very close to the enemy at `(3,4)` and `(5,4)`.
            Wait, TF-1 members listed: `e` (5,2), `f` (5,3), `g` (4,2), `h` (4,4). `c` (5,0), `d` (5,1). That's 6 armies. Plus a transport in TF-3?
            No, Map says: `n #9 transport (7,2) ... TF-3`. So TF-1 has no transport currently.
            The TF-1 tasking says "awaiting second transport". But the enemy just landed 2 armies nearby.
            If I continue STAGE (5,2), they might not be able to fight because there are no transports? No, TF-3 is on `(7,2)`. They can't stage at `(5,2)` if that's their objective but the threat is immediate.
            Actually, I need to check my cities for production.
            (4,1) is building TRANSPORT. It took 29 turns. That's a big one.
            (4,3) has `l` and `m`. Wait, `m` is "NEW t52". So the enemy just landed there? No, `(4,3)` IS MY CITY (building ARMY). Why are two UNASSIGNED armies at `(4,3)`? Ah, `k` at `(2,0)`, `l` at `(4,3)`. Both in cities.
            My strategy: Defend the breach at column 5.
            TF-1 is too exposed or already right there. TF-1 tasking says "STAGE (5,2)". If they are already staging at that point, maybe they need to change objective to CAPTURE/DEFEND?
            But rule: "Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first". Uncommitted units: `k` (in city 2,0?), `l` (in city 4,3), `m` (in city 4,3), `i` (1,0), `j` (3,0).
            Also Rule: "Capture attempts consume armies... Assault with spares".
            Since the enemy just landed at `(3,4)` and `(5,4)`, they are a new threat.
            TF-3 is too far away to help immediately with transport `n`. But maybe I can use TF-3 for reinforcement? No, `TF-3` tasking is "PATROL (7,2)". It has a destroyer escorting a transport.
            The enemy transport `Z` is empty at `(4,5)`. Wait, "empty". The one that dropped armies is sunk or used. If it's empty, the units left behind are captured? No, if they unloaded 2 armies, those armies were moved from water to land. The transport went to `(4,5)` (empty).
            So my priority: Defend the column 4-5 area.
            TF-1 is right there. Their tasking says "STAGE". I should probably amend TF-1's order to DEFEND or CAPTURE? No, rule says "Capture attempts consume armies... Assault with spares". They are at `(5,2)`, enemy at `(5,4)`. They can capture if they push `(5,4)` into `(4,4)` or similar. But I must output the amendment.
            The order forms are `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>` and `TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>` and `TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>`.
            I must select one form per TF (usually), but can mix REINFORCE + RETASK.
            I should probably move TF-2 to help if possible? TF-2 is at `(2,0)` garrisoning capital. It's a good place to keep it for now unless the enemy is threatening there. `k` (#13 army) is UNASSIGNED but inside city (2,0).
            Wait, `k` is listed under `UNASSIGNED`. `TF-2` is at `(2,0)` with `a` and `b`. So there are 3 units in my capital? Or maybe `k` hasn't joined yet.
            The immediate threat is the enemy armies `X`(3,4) and `Y`(5,4).
            I should order TF-1 to RETASK DEFEND or CAPTURE. But TF-1 has no transport to move across water if they need to cross? No, `(5,2)` is land (likely). The map `~` indicates water. `(5,2)` is surrounded by `h(4,2)` and `Y(5,4)`. It seems like a valid staging spot for armies.
            Actually, the order is "STAGE (5,2)". I should probably keep that or change to DEFEND? If they are "awaiting second transport", maybe they just need to wait. But why await? The enemy landed.
            Let's check TF-3. It has a destroyer and transport at `(7,2)`. `n` (transport) is empty? No, list says `empty`. Why is it empty? Did they just unload? If the transport is empty, can we reload? "To send armies across: put the armies + a transport... in ONE task force".
            If TF-3 transport is empty, it's useless unless I have armies to load.
            Wait, list says `TF-3 members ... n #9 transport (7,2) empty`.
            So TF-3 has 1 empty transport. It can't move armies across water yet because there are none on board.
            However, I have unassigned armies: `k(2,0), l(4,3), m(4,3), i(1,0), j(3,0)`.
            The rule says: "Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first... not at the nearest garrison".
            TF-2 is the garrison (2,0). I should not pull from there.
            TF-1 is already near the enemy. `a` and `b` (TF-2) are in capital `(2,0)`. `k` is inside that same city but marked UNASSIGNED? That's odd.
            Wait, looking at Map r0: `a #1 army`. List says `a TF-2`. Map r0 also shows `i #3`? No, `i` is a marker in map at `(1,0)`. Unit list says `i #11 army (1,0) UNASSIGNED`. Okay, so `k` and `l` and `m` are unassigned reserves.
            I should probably REINFORCE TF-1 to help them with the immediate threat? They already have 6 armies.
            Maybe I can order TF-3 to REINFORCE UNITS `n`? No, `REINFORCE` adds units to a TF. `TF-3` needs more fighting power or capacity? It's stuck at `(7,2)` with an empty transport.
            Better idea: Use the uncommitted armies to form a new Task Force that can engage the enemy directly without staging.
            Or: I should order TF-1 to RETASK CAPTURE (4,4) or something closer? But "Capture attempts consume armies". `Y` is at `(5,4)`. `h` is at `(4,4)`. They are adjacent.
            I will order TF-1 to RETASK DEFEND `(5,4)`? Or CAPTURE `(5,4)`. The enemy army there is X or Y. `Y` is at `(5,4)`.
            If I don't move TF-2, where do I get strength?
            Let's check production. My City (4,3) is building ARMY (Turn 51/52 left?). `(4,3)` has 2 armies (`l`, `m`). Production is building ARMY. I need more units to counter the landing.
            But I should order TF-1 to RETASK DEFEND (or CAPTURE). Since they are staging there, changing to CAPTURE makes sense to stop enemy expansion. "Conquest is won by taking cities". The enemy just landed armies on `(3,4)` and `(5,4)`. They need to be stopped or removed.
            Wait, rule: "A unit moves up to its speed...". Army `h` (4,4) vs Enemy `Y` (5,4). Str 1 each. It's a fight.
            I should order TF-3 to help? TF-3 has a transport. If the enemy is on land `(5,4)`, sea units don't move there.
            Actually, looking at the map: `X Y` are next to `h`. `TF-1` is holding the line. `a` and `b` (TF-2) are way back.
            I should order TF-3 to RETASK REINFORCE UNITS...? No, TF-3 needs an objective to move where? `STAGE`?
            Wait, "A force cannot hold your cities and conquer the enemy's at once."
            The prompt says "Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first".
            Uncommitted: `k(2,0) in city`, `l(4,3) in city`. Why are they inside my cities if TF-1 needs to move? Maybe they can't leave cities yet (unless the rule allows). Rule doesn't explicitly forbid moving units from a city. It says "Army captures by entering".
            Actually, I should probably order TF-1 to RETASK CAPTURE `(5,4)` or `STAGE` again if it's better to push them away? No, capture consumes armies.
            Wait, `TF-1` tasking: "awaiting second transport before striking east". If I change this to "CAPTURE (5,4)" without a transport, it violates the doctrine of staging. But TF-1 has land armies there.
            Let's assume TF-1 can engage on land directly. 6 Armies is strong. `TF-1` is already at `(5,2)`. `Y` is at `(5,4)`. They are within range (scan 1 for Army).
            So I will order TF-1 to RETASK CAPTURE `(5,4)` or DEFEND `(5,4)`. Given the threat just arrived, DEFEND might be better until production builds more strength. But "press outward while you can".
            I'll go with `CAPTURE (5,4)` but add a REINFORCE line if needed? No, I have 6 armies already there. That's plenty to contest a capture attempt (needs spares).
            What about TF-3? It has a transport at `(7,2)`. It needs an objective. Since the enemy transport is empty, and enemy armies are on land, TF-3's transport isn't useful for movement unless it carries my armies. But my armies are in the field or staging areas.
            Wait, I have `j` (3,0) and `i` (1,0). They are UNASSIGNED.
            Maybe I should FORM a new TF with them to patrol?
            No, the output is strict: "Every standing TF gets exactly one line... DISBAND... FORM... REINFORCE".
            I must issue orders for existing TAs first.
            Existing TAs: TF-1, TF-2, TF-3.
            TF-1: Needs to react to landing. RETASK CAPTURE (5,4) seems most aggressive and consistent with "Conquest is won by taking cities".
            TF-2: "DEFEND (2,0)". The threat is at column 5/6. This remains a valid tasking. I can REINFORCE it? `TF-2` has only land armies near capital. Maybe I should use unassigned units to support it if they are close enough?
            TF-3: "PATROL (7,2)". Transport is empty. It might need to move to intercept the enemy transport `Z` at `(4,5)`? But it can't sail on water without a cargo or specific movement rules? No, sea units move on water. Destroyer + Transport moves. If it's just a patrol, keep it there. But the enemy transport is empty.
            Actually, TF-1 has `h` at `(4,4)`. Enemy `Y` at `(5,4)`. That's a direct confrontation zone. I should tell them to engage or move closer?
            "Naming a unit's destination tile means you've dropped too low." -> No destinations.
            But RETASK CAPTURE <target> implies the objective is targeting that specific city/position? "CAPTURE <city (x,y)>". `(5,4)` is not a MY city. It's land occupied by enemy army. Can I capture an empty tile there? Or does it mean capture the *unit* there?
            The contract says: `CAPTURE <city (x,y)>`. This implies taking control of the tile/city. The prompt says "Enemy holds zero cities". If `(5,4)` is not a city, capturing a unit there means neutralizing the threat.
